CREATE TABLE test_table (id INT, value INT);
INSERT INTO test_table VALUES (1, NULL), (2, 10), (3, 20), (4, NULL), (5, 30);
SELECT count(value) FROM test_table WHERE id > 1;
SELECT count(value) FROM test_table WHERE value IS NOT NULL;
CREATE TABLE another_table (id INT, test_id INT, description VARCHAR(100));
INSERT INTO another_table VALUES (1, 1, 'Test 1'), (2, 1, 'Test 2'), (3, 2, NULL), (4, NULL, 'Test 4');
SELECT test_table.id, count(another_table.id) FROM test_table LEFT JOIN another_table ON test_table.id = another_table.test_id GROUP BY test_table.id;
SELECT count(*) FROM (SELECT value FROM test_table WHERE value IS NOT NULL) AS non_null_values;
CREATE TABLE yet_another_table (group_id INT, metric_value INT);
INSERT INTO yet_another_table VALUES (1, NULL), (1, 5), (1, 8), (2, 10), (2, NULL);
SELECT group_id, count(metric_value) FROM yet_another_table GROUP BY group_id;
SELECT t1.id, count(t2.id) AS count_desc FROM test_table t1 JOIN another_table t2 ON t1.id = t2.test_id GROUP BY t1.id;
CREATE TABLE complex_table (category INT, item_value INT);
SELECT category, count(item_value) FROM complex_table GROUP BY category HAVING count(item_value) > 0;
SELECT count(*) FROM (SELECT id FROM test_table WHERE id < 5 UNION SELECT test_id FROM another_table WHERE test_id IS NOT NULL) AS combined;
SELECT count(value) FROM test_table WHERE value BETWEEN 10 AND 30;
